title: Regular expressions should not contain empty groups
description: Empty groups in regular expressions (i.e., '()') serve no purpose and can make the pattern harder to read and maintain. They may also indicate a mistake or misunderstanding in the regex logic.
recommendation: Remove empty groups from regular expressions to improve clarity and avoid confusion.
impact: Code with unnecessary empty groups in regex patterns is harder to understand and maintain, and may hide logical errors.

examples.bad: Pattern.compile("a()b"); // Noncompliant: contains an empty group

checks.message: Remove empty groups from regular expressions to improve clarity and avoid confusion.
